name: Device Energy & P2P Power Management
description: Sub-sector template for device-level power management, energy harvesting, P2P/sidelink power allocation, IoT power efficiency, and battery/energy storage integration patents.
contextDescription: Device-level energy management covers power state transitions beyond DRX, including deep sleep, hibernation, and energy harvesting. P2P/sidelink power allocation is important for D2D and relay scenarios. IoT protocols (NB-IoT, LTE-M, BLE) have extreme power constraints — devices must last years on a single battery. RF energy harvesting and wireless power transfer are emerging areas. Broadcom's IoT and connectivity chipsets (Wi-Fi, BLE, Zigbee) all implement device power state machines. Key targets: IoT chipset vendors, smartphone SoC vendors, wearable manufacturers.
scoringGuidance: IoT power efficiency patents have massive device count: billions of IoT endpoints need multi-year battery life.
Power state management patents are exercised by every device with sleep/wake capability.
Energy harvesting patents are forward-looking but increasingly relevant for batteryless IoT.
P2P/sidelink power patents target V2X and relay — growing importance with Rel-17/18.
Battery management integration patents bridge the gap between wireless and power electronics.

questions.question: Does this patent address energy harvesting for wireless devices? Score 8-10 for RF energy harvesting, wireless power transfer, or ambient energy integration with communication. 5-7 for energy-aware protocol design considering harvested energy. 3-4 for general energy awareness. 1-2 for no energy harvesting aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: RF energy harvesting enables batteryless IoT devices — emerging technology with high future value.

questions.question: Does this patent manage device power state transitions (active, sleep, deep sleep, hibernation, off)? Score 8-10 for novel multi-level power state machines or conditional state transitions. 5-7 for improved sleep/wake procedures beyond standard DRX. 3-4 for basic power state management. 1-2 for no power state innovation.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Device power state machines are implemented in every wireless chipset — universal infringement surface.

questions.question: Does this patent address power efficiency for IoT protocols (NB-IoT, LTE-M, LoRa, BLE, Zigbee)? Score 8-10 for protocol-specific power optimization enabling multi-year battery life. 5-7 for general IoT power-aware communication design. 3-4 for IoT mentioned but not power-specific. 1-2 for no IoT protocol relevance.
questions.reasoningPrompt: NB-IoT and BLE power optimization patents cover billions of deployed IoT devices.

questions.question: Does this patent integrate with battery management IC, charge cycle optimization, or power delivery? Score 8-10 for novel battery-communication co-design or adaptive charging based on communication patterns. 5-7 for battery-aware protocol behavior. 3-4 for general battery considerations. 1-2 for no battery technology aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Battery management IC integration bridges wireless and power electronics — relevant for Broadcom's PMIC portfolio.

questions.question: Does this patent address D2D/sidelink power allocation or relay power management? Score 8-10 for novel sidelink power control or relay-specific power optimization. 5-7 for D2D-aware power management. 3-4 for general P2P with power consideration. 1-2 for no peer-to-peer power aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Sidelink power control is critical for V2X reliability — power must be managed without network assistance.

questions.question: Is this device energy management observable through device behavior or protocol analysis? Score 8-10 for behavior visible in device power profiles, protocol traces, or radio measurements. 5-7 for behavior inferable from device performance characteristics. 3-4 for internal chipset power management. 1-2 for algorithm-level design only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Device power behavior is measurable through current profiling and protocol analysis tools.
